highlights.title: OpenAI Proposes Giving the US Government a 5% Stake, FT Says
highlights.description: OpenAI has begun preliminary discussions about giving the US government a 5% stake in the ChatGPT-developer, the Financial Times reported, citing two people familiar with the talks.

highlights.title: OpenAI reportedly wants all AI companies to give the US government a stake in their businesses
highlights.description: According to the Financial Times, Sam Altman is floating the idea of having leading AI companies give US sovereign funds a five percent equity.

highlights.title: Google loses fight over record $4.7 billion EU antitrust fine
highlights.description: In 2018, the European Commission slapped Google with the record-breaking penalty on the grounds that it abused Android's mobile dominance.

highlights.title: SpaceX Analyst Debut Set to Test $2.2 Trillion Valuation
highlights.description: Investors in SpaceX have been largely flying blind since the company’s record-breaking IPO last month, with few financial projections to help determine what the stock is actually worth.

highlights.title: Google Loses EU Top Court Fight Over €4.1 Billion Android Fine
highlights.description: Google lost its long-running fight against a €4.1 billion ($4.7 billion) European Union antitrust fine after the bloc’s top judges said regulators were right to punish the US giant for abusing Android’s market power.

highlights.title: Google loses final appeal over $4.7 billion EU Android antitrust fine
highlights.description: Europe's highest court of appeal has upheld a record-setting $4.7 billion fine imposed on Google back in 2018.

highlights.title: Shares of BYD and Xiaomi surge as June delivery figures fuel optimism
highlights.description: Hong Kong-listed shares of Chinese electric vehicle makers surged, after companies posted June vehicle data that helped to buoy investor sentiment.

highlights.title: ShareChat, India’s Meta Rival, Plans $400 Million IPO Next Year
highlights.description: Mohalla Tech Pvt., which operates social network ShareChat, short-video platform Moj and subscription-based micro-drama app QuickTV, is seeking to raise as much as $400 million from a public market listing planned for next year.

highlights.title: OpenAI proposes 5% stake to Trump administration to ease Washington pressure: report
highlights.description: Trump said in June that the U.S. taking an ownership stake in AI giants would be "a beautiful thing" and make American public "partners in this revolution."

highlights.title: Indian tech tycoon bets $30M of his own money to build AI alternative to Microsoft Office
highlights.description: Neo is Bhavin Turakhia’s fifth venture and his latest involving enterprise software. This time he's taking on Microsoft Office, Google Apps with AI.

ai.title: Indian tech tycoon bets $30M of his own money to build AI alternative to Microsoft Office
ai.description: Neo is Bhavin Turakhia’s fifth venture and his latest involving enterprise software. This time he's taking on Microsoft Office, Google Apps with AI.

ai.title: Musk-Altman Judge Enlists Mediator to Resolve AI Titans’ Battle
ai.description: The judge overseeing the legal battle between Elon Musk and Sam Altman and their dueling AI startups enlisted a mediator to resolve the dispute.

ai.title: Amazon is designing its own AI chips for Echo, Fire TV and future devices, exec tells CNBC
ai.description: Amazon hardware chief Panos Panay says the company is designing custom chips for key devices as it experiments with AI gadgets.

ai.title: Chip Stocks Slump Across Asia as Global Tech Selloff Intensifies
ai.description: Asian semiconductor stocks tumbled on Thursday, tracking tech losses on Wall Street after Meta Platforms Inc.’s plan to develop a business that would sell access to AI computing power raised worries about overcapacity.

ai.title: Kospi Falls as Chip Selloff Spreads
ai.description: Stocks fell as a selloff in chip stocks spread to South Korea, reviving concerns the rally in artificial intelligence shares may have gone too far. Bloomberg's Anthony Stephens reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

ai.title: Korea Chip Selloff Leads Equities Lower
ai.description: Stocks fell as a selloff in chip stocks spread to South Korea, reviving concerns the rally in artificial intelligence shares may have gone too far, too fast. Bloomberg's Avril Hong reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

ai.title: Nvidia Offers Revenue Sharing Model for Aspiring AI Startups
ai.description: Nvidia Corp. is looking to expand access to its leading AI hardware by granting token credits to developers in exchange for a share of their future sales.

ai.title: Former Goldman Analyst’s AI Startup Is Said to Raise $22 Million
ai.description: LinqAlpha Inc., an artificial intelligence investment research company, is close to raising $22 million in a Series A funding round, according to people familiar with the matter.

ai.title: SAP Restricts Hiring, Travel to Fund ‘Significant’ AI Push
ai.description: SAP SE said it will cut back hiring and travel to save costs as Europe’s largest software company devotes more resources to developing artificial intelligence technologies and fending off new competitors.

ai.title: GMI Cloud CEO on Business Strategy, AI Ecosystem
ai.description: Alex Yeh, Founder and CEO at GMI Cloud, discusses the company's business strategy and the outlook for the AI ecosystem. He speaks with Shery Ahn from the sidelines of the IVS2026 conference in Kyoto. (Source: Bloomberg)

ai.title: Apple to ask court to halt Epic Games proceedings pending Supreme Court ruling
ai.description: Apple and Epic Games have agreed to ask the court to temporarily postpone key deadlines in their long-running App Store dispute while Apple seeks to pause the proceedings until the Supreme Court rules on its appeal. Here are the details.

regulation.title: Google loses fight over record $4.7 billion EU antitrust fine
regulation.description: In 2018, the European Commission slapped Google with the record-breaking penalty on the grounds that it abused Android's mobile dominance.

regulation.title: Google Loses EU Top Court Fight Over €4.1 Billion Android Fine
regulation.description: Google lost its long-running fight against a €4.1 billion ($4.7 billion) European Union antitrust fine after the bloc’s top judges said regulators were right to punish the US giant for abusing Android’s market power.

regulation.title: Google loses final appeal over $4.7 billion EU Android antitrust fine
regulation.description: Europe's highest court of appeal has upheld a record-setting $4.7 billion fine imposed on Google back in 2018.

business.title: SpaceX Analyst Debut Set to Test $2.2 Trillion Valuation
business.description: Investors in SpaceX have been largely flying blind since the company’s record-breaking IPO last month, with few financial projections to help determine what the stock is actually worth.

business.title: India M&A Funding Rule to Squeeze Private Credit, Moody’s Says
business.description: India’s decision allowing banks to fund acquisitions can potentially hurt private credit funds as it would squeeze returns in one of their most lucrative businesses, Moody’s Ratings said.

business.title: Data Center Firm Switch Seeks $2 Billion in Funding Round
business.description: Switch Inc. is kicking off a private funding round led by Andreessen Horowitz that could raise about $2 billion, according to people familiar with the matter.

ev_autonomous.title: Shares of BYD and Xiaomi surge as June delivery figures fuel optimism
ev_autonomous.description: Hong Kong-listed shares of Chinese electric vehicle makers surged, after companies posted June vehicle data that helped to buoy investor sentiment.

ev_autonomous.title: Metaplanet buys another $170 million of bitcoin expanding treasury to 43,000 BTC
ev_autonomous.description: The Japanese firm, now the world's third largest publicly traded bitcoin holder, reported stronger Bitcoin Income Generation revenue.

ev_autonomous.title: Meta Is Charging a Subscription for Smart Glasses Features. Welcome to the New Era of Consumer Tech
ev_autonomous.description: You bought the hardware. Now you’ll need to subscribe for “expanded access” to the most advanced features.

ev_autonomous.title: Apple reportedly orders 10M foldable iPhone Ultra models, which could sell for around $2500
ev_autonomous.description: Apple plans to manufacture and sell around 10 million foldable iPhone Ultra models, according to a new report. That’s roughly a third more than the average build targets previously expected.

space.title: Amazon has enough satellites to launch its Starlink competitor
space.description:
Payloads of Amazon’s Leo satellites sorted by launch vehicle. | Image: Amazon	

Amazon says it now has enough satellites operating in low-Earth orbit to light up its Starlink internet competitor. With last night's launch, Amazon Leo has 396  satellites deployed, which is "enough to support continuous service across initial latitudes," according to Chris Weber, VP heading up business and product for Amazon Leo. That puts the company on track to meet its "mid-2026" target for commercial availability. Just don't expect miracles on day one.
SpaceX went live with its "Better than nothing beta" back in 2020 when it had almost 900 satellites operating in low-Earth orbit. It initially served a narrow band of users in the upper …
Read the full story at The Verge.

chips_cloud.title: Apple Lobbies US to Buy Chinese-Made Memory Chips
chips_cloud.description: Apple Inc. is in negotiations to purchase chips from Chinese semiconductor makers ChangXin Memory Technologies Inc. and Yangtze Memory Technologies Co. to help reduce the impact of a global memory shortage. Bloomberg's Minmin Low has the context. (Source: Bloomberg)

chips_cloud.title: SoftBank Plans AI Cloud Services in US to Tap Surging Demand
chips_cloud.description: SoftBank Group Corp. and its telecom unit will start renting AI computing resources to US companies next fiscal year in a bid to capitalize on the company’s growing pipeline of data center projects.

chips_cloud.title: Lombard Odier Urges Tech Exposure 'At the Market Weight'
chips_cloud.description: Lombard Odier's EMEA CIO Nannette Hechler-Fayd'Herbe discusses the outlook for tech stocks, volatility and where she sees opportunities beyond artificial intelligence and semiconductors. She speaks on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)
